description:
You are a QA engineer who has just received an automatically-generated textual report from an overnight regression run.  
    The file already exists on the machine and is located at  
        /home/user/reports/test_report.txt  

    • The report is UTF-8 plain text.  
    • Each non-blank line follows this exact pattern (three fields separated by a colon + one blank after each colon):  
            TC-<4-digit-id> : <STATUS> : <DURATION_ms>  
      where  
        – <STATUS> is either PASS or FAIL (uppercase, no other values),  
        – <DURATION_ms> is an integer giving elapsed time in milliseconds.  
      Example of a real line from the report:  
            TC-0007 : FAIL : 1452  

    Your objective is to generate a **single-line** summary file so that downstream tooling can ingest it without additional parsing logic.  
    1. Read /home/user/reports/test_report.txt.  
    2. Count every non-blank line – that is the total number of executed test cases.  
    3. Count only the lines whose status field is FAIL – that is the total number of failed tests.  
    4. Create a new file at exactly  
           /home/user/reports/test_summary.txt  
       (overwrite it if it already exists).  
    5. Write **one single line** in the following strict format (case-sensitive, no extra spaces, no trailing blank line):  

           TOTAL=<total_tests>;FAILED=<failed_tests>

       Example when 12 tests ran and 3 failed:  
           TOTAL=12;FAILED=3  

    6. Leave the terminating newline (ASCII 0x0A) after that line – the automated checker expects exactly one newline at end-of-file.  
    7. Complete the task using exactly **one** awk or sed command (pipelines are fine, but no additional scripting languages).  

    After you finish, /home/user/reports/test_summary.txt must exist and contain the line in the required format followed by a single newline. The automated test harness will read the file to verify your work.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing file that the agent will read -->
    <initialFile path="/home/user/reports/test_report.txt"><![CDATA[
TC-0001 : PASS : 1120
TC-0002 : FAIL : 2075
TC-0003 : PASS : 998
TC-0004 : PASS : 1311
TC-0005 : FAIL : 1567
TC-0006 : PASS : 1044
TC-0007 : FAIL : 1452
TC-0008 : PASS : 1005
TC-0009 : PASS : 1113
TC-0010 : PASS : 1250
]]></initialFile>

    <!-- Expected file after the agent completes the task -->
    <expectedFile path="/home/user/reports/test_summary.txt"><![CDATA[
TOTAL=10;FAILED=3
]]></expectedFile>
